title: Reducing avoidable transmissions of electronic message content
abstract: Techniques for electronic messaging including receiving a first request to send a first attachment to a first recipient body with a first message, receiving a second request from an end-user device to send a second attachment to a second recipient body with a second message, obtaining a first fingerprint for the second attachment, determining that the first fingerprint matches a second fingerprint for the first attachment, determining, based on the first fingerprint matching the second fingerprint, that the end-user device does not need to provide the second attachment, and indicating to the end-user device, in response to the determination that the second end-user device does not need to provide the second attachment, that the end-user device does not need to transmit the second message attachment to another device in association with the second message.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of electronic messaging, the method comprising: receiving, at a first computer system from a first end-user computing device via a data communication network at a first time, a first request to send a first user-indicated message attachment to a first recipient body in association with a request to send a first message to the first recipient body; receiving, at the first computer system from a second end-user computing device via a data communication network after the first time, a second request to send a second user-indicated message attachment to a second recipient body in association with a request to send a second message to the second recipient body; obtaining, at the first computer system, a first fingerprint resulting from a first fingerprinting algorithm being applied to the second user-indicated message attachment; determining that the first fingerprint matches a second fingerprint resulting from the first fingerprinting algorithm being applied to the first user-indicated message attachment; determining, by the first computer system based on the determination that the first fingerprint matches the second fingerprint, that the second end-user computing device does not need to provide the second user-indicated message attachment; and indicating to the second end-user computing device, in response to the determination that the second end-user computing device does not need to provide the second user-indicated message attachment, that the second end-user computing device does not need to transmit the second user-indicated message attachment to another computing device in association with the second message. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: obtaining, at the first computer system, the second fingerprint; recording, by the first computer system, a first association between the second fingerprint and a first user or a communication group corresponding to the first recipient body; and determining that the first user or the communication group corresponds to the second recipient body, wherein the determination that the second end-user computing device does not need to provide the second user-indicated message attachment is based on the determination that the first fingerprint matches the second fingerprint, the determination that the first user or the communication group corresponds to the second recipient body, 

 and the recorded first association between the second fingerprint and the first user or the communication group. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising, in response to the receipt of the second request: identifying a third end-user computing device associated with the second recipient body; and transmitting, by the first computer system to the third end-user computing device, a notification that the second user-indicated message attachment was sent is association with the second message, the notification providing a sample image of a content of the second user-indicated message attachment, wherein the sample image has a smaller transmission size than the second user-indicated message attachment. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising: causing, by an indication transmitted by the first computer system to the first end- user computing device via a data communication network, the first end-user computing device to transmit the first user-indicated message attachment to a second computing device via a data communication network; causing a first attachment data to be stored, the first attachment data generated based on the first user-indicated message attachment transmitted from the first end-user computing device to the second computing device; identifying a third end-user computing device corresponding to the second recipient body; transmitting, by the first computer system, the second message to the third end- user computing device; and transmitting, by the first computer system, a notification to the third end-user computing device that the second user-indicated message attachment was sent in association with the second message, the notification including a first identifier for use by the third end-user computing device to obtain a third message attachment generated from the stored first attachment data. 5. The method of claim 1, further comprising determining that the first fingerprint matches the second fingerprint based on the first fingerprint being identical to the second fingerprint or a similarity metric between the first fingerprint and the second fingerprint satisfying a threshold condition. 6. A computer system for electronic messaging, the computer system comprising: a processing unit including a processor configured to execute instructions and 

 process data; a communication component operable to couple to the processing unit to a first data communication network; and a machine-readable medium including first instructions with, when executed by the processing unit, cause the computer system to: receive, from a first end-user computing device via the first data communication network at a first time, a first request to send a first user-indicated message attachment to a first recipient body in association with a request to send a first message to the first recipient body; receive, from a second end-user computing device via a data communication network after the first time, a second request to send a second user- indicated message attachment to a second recipient body in association with a request to send a second message to the second recipient body; obtain a first fingerprint resulting from a first fingerprinting algorithm being applied to the second user-indicated message attachment; determine that the first fingerprint matches a second fingerprint resulting from the first fingerprinting algorithm being applied to the first user-indicated message attachment; determine, based on the determination that the first fingerprint matches the second fingerprint, that the second end-user computing device does not need to provide the second user-indicated message attachment; and indicate to the second end-user computing device, in response to the determination that the second end-user computing device does not need to provide the second user-indicated message attachment, that the second end-user computing device does not need to transmit the second user-indicated message attachment to another computing device in association with the second message. 7. The computer system of claim 6, wherein the first instructions further cause the computer system to receive the first fingerprint from the second end-user computing device in association with the second request. 8. The computer system of claim 6, wherein the first instructions further cause the computer system to: obtain the second fingerprint; record a first association between the second fingerprint and a first user or a communication group corresponding to the first recipient body; and 

 determine that the first user or the communication group corresponds to the second recipient body, wherein the first instructions cause the computer system to determine that the second end-user computing device does not need to provide the second user-indicated message attachment based on the determination that the first fingerprint matches the second fingerprint, the determination that the first user or the communication group corresponds to the second recipient body, and the recorded first association between the second fingerprint and the first user or the communication group. 9. The computer system of claim 6, wherein the first instructions further cause the computer system to: cause, by an indication transmitted to the first end-user computing device via a data communication network, the first end-user computing device to transmit the first user-indicated message attachment to a second computing device via a data communication network; cause a first attachment data to be stored, the first attachment data generated based on the first user-indicated message attachment transmitted from the first end-user computing device to the second computing device; identify a third end-user computing device corresponding to the second recipient body; transmit the second message to the third end-user computing device; and transmitting a notification to the third end-user computing device that the second user-indicated message attachment was sent in association with the second message, the notification including a first identifier for use by the third end-user computing device to obtain a third message attachment generated from the stored first attachment data. 10. The computer system of claim 6, wherein the first instructions further cause the computer system to determine that the first fingerprint matches the second fingerprint based on the first fingerprint being identical to the second fingerprint or a similarity metric between the first fingerprint and the second fingerprint satisfying a threshold condition. 11. The method of claim 1, further comprising receiving the first fingerprint at the first computer system from the second end-user computing device in association with the second request. 12. The method of claim 1, further comprising: identifying, by the first computer system, a third end-user computing device corresponding to the second recipient body; 

 transmitting the obtained first fingerprint from the first computer system to the identified third end-user computing device; and receiving, by the first computer system, a first indication from the third end-user computing device that the first fingerprint matches a fingerprint of a message attachment previously sent to the third end-user computing device or a recipient user corresponding to the second recipient body, wherein the determination that the second end-user computing device does not need to provide the second user-indicated message attachment is based on the first indication being received by the first computer system from the third end-user computing device. 13. The method of claim 5, wherein the first fingerprinting algorithm generates a fingerprint based on a perceptual hash value produced by a first perceptual hashing function for at least a portion of a message attachment to which the first fingerprinting algorithm is being applied. 14. The computer system of claim 6, wherein the first instructions further cause the computer system to, in response to the receipt of the second request: identify a third end-user computing device associated with the second recipient body; and transmit, to the third end-user computing device, a notification that the second user-indicated message attachment was sent is association with the second message, the notification providing a sample image of a content of the second user-indicated message attachment, wherein the sample image has a smaller transmission size than the second user-indicated message attachment. 15. The computer system of claim 6, wherein: the second computing device is included in the computer system; and the first instructions further cause the computer system to: receive the transmission of the first user-indicated message attachment from the first end-user computing device, receive a third request, from the third end-user computing device for attachment data corresponding to the first identifier, and transmit, in response receiving the third request, the third message attachment generated from the stored first attachment data to the third end-user computing device via a data communication network.
